summary: Decompiled and matched two ftPopo SpecialS/SpecialHi air physics functions: ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_1_Phys and ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_1_Phys. The implementations share the same air-fall pattern: get Fighter and ftIceClimberAttributes, preserve stack shape with PAD_STACK(8), call ftCommon_Fall using newly named attributes xA8 and xAC, then call ftCommon_8007CEF4 when vertical velocity is negative. The PR also refined ftIceClimberAttributes by replacing padding at 0xA8-0xB0 with two float fields.
what_changed: Replaced the nonmatching marker for ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_1_Phys with a C implementation.
Replaced the nonmatching marker for ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_1_Phys with a C implementation.
Both newly matched physics functions call ftCommon_Fall(fp, da->xA8, da->xAC).
Both functions check fp->self_vel.y < 0.0f and call ftCommon_8007CEF4(fp) when falling downward.
types.h changed ftIceClimberAttributes padding at offsets 0xA8 and 0xAC into explicit float fields xA8 and xAC.
The PR body reports a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions.
smart_moves: Identified that the two air physics functions are structurally identical except for fighter access style in the diff: ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_1_Phys uses gobj->user_data while ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_1_Phys uses GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Promoted previously opaque ftIceClimberAttributes padding into float fields only where needed by matched code, preserving the rest of the unknown struct layout.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in both functions, indicating attention to stack layout required for matching rather than only source-level behavior.
Reused common fighter physics helpers instead of open-coding gravity/fall behavior: ftCommon_Fall and ftCommon_8007CEF4.
decomp_lessons: When an air physics function calls ftCommon_Fall with fighter-specific attributes, previously unknown attribute bytes may need to be split into float fields rather than left as padding.
Matching small fighter physics functions can depend on preserving otherwise semantically irrelevant stack padding with PAD_STACK.
Nearby ground and air variants can provide the control-flow template for matching related action-state physics callbacks.
Do not over-name newly exposed attribute fields without stronger semantic evidence; this PR kept them as offset-style names xA8 and xAC.
naming_conventions: Kept ftIceClimberAttributes fields as offset-based names xA8 and xAC because their precise semantic names were not established in the PR evidence.
Function names follow the existing ftPp action callback convention: ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_1_Phys and ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_1_Phys.
The struct type remains character-specific as ftIceClimberAttributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in both matched functions to satisfy stack-sensitive matching.
Used ftCommon_Fall(fp, da->xA8, da->xAC) as the core physics call.
Used a signed float comparison against 0.0f on fp->self_vel.y before calling ftCommon_8007CEF4(fp).
Matched both functions to 100.0 fuzzy match according to the PR summary.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human-text verification evidence says ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent is 100.0 for all functions.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftIceClimberAttributes users of xA8 and xAC to infer semantic names for these float fields.
Search for other fighter air physics functions using ftCommon_Fall followed by ftCommon_8007CEF4 when self_vel.y is negative.
Compare Popo and Nana SpecialAirHi physics callbacks for equivalent struct field usage and naming opportunities.
Check whether GET_FIGHTER versus gobj->user_data is required for matching in nearby ftPopo functions or was inherited from local style.
